channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Nevada Senate hopefuls tackle trans athletes, immigration and UFOs in only debate
item.description: Sen. Jackie Rosen and Republican candidate Sam Brown went head to head in the Nevada Senate debate on immigration, the economy and biological males competing in women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Republicans mainstreamed the baseless idea of noncitizen voting in 2024
item.description: Donald Trump and his allies have zeroed in on the baseless claim that Democrats are encouraging newly arrived migrants to vote for them in the 2024 election. There is no evidence of a plot like this.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block and Miles Parks, MPR News

item.title: Five races for the Republican-dominated State Board of Education to watch this year
item.description: Revising the social studies curriculum could be among the divisive issues the Texas State Board of Education tackles next year.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jaden Edison, KPRC 2

item.title: Walz says 90-year-old mom lives off social security check during North Carolina rally
item.description: During his first of two campaign stops in North Carolina, Minnesota Governor Tim Walz claimed that his mother has to depend on her social security checks each month to survive.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At Wisconsin stops, Harris campaign features less talk of joy and more attacks on Trump
item.description: In La Crosse on Thursday, Harris told a rally crowd that Trump “will stop at nothing to claim unchecked power for himself.” It’s a shift from the “joy” that swirled around her elevation to the top of the Democratic ticket this summer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Harris campaign features less talk of joy and more head-on digs at Trump as Election Day nears
item.description: LA CROSSE, Wis. (AP) — Joy Olson proudly wore a “Make America Joyful Again” button Thursday as she waited in line to attend a Kamala Harris rally.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump takes jabs at Harris during keynote at Al Smith dinner, vows to correct 'serious problems' in US
item.description: Former President Trump will headline the Al Smith Catholic charity dinner in New York on Thursday, after Vice President Kamala Harris snubbed the event and will send a video message instead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Illinois alderwoman claims she was arrested after criticizing the mayor
item.description: Alderwoman Colby Chapman said she spent five hours in jail, in what she calls a politically motivated abuse of power.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, NewsNation

item.title: WATCH: Nevada US Senate Debate: Jacky Rosen and Sam Brown
item.description: As the seconds tick away until Election Day on Nov. 5, Democratic incumbent Sen. Jacky Rosen and Republican challenger Sam Brown are set to debate the issues Nevadans are most concerned about heading into 2025 and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Following death of Hamas leader, Harris says it's 'time for the day after to begin' in Gaza
item.description: Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris says the death of Hamas’ top leader, Yahya Sinwar, “gives us an opportunity to finally end the war in Gaza.”.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Colleen Long, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump delivers a pointed and at times bitter speech at Al Smith charity dinner
item.description: Former President Donald Trump laced into Vice President Kamala Harris and other Democrats in a pointed and at times bitter speech as he headlined the annual Al Smith charity dinner in New York.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: GOP cringes over Trump’s vow to wield military against opponents
item.description: Former President Trump’s incendiary comments about deploying the military and using other powers of the executive branch to pursue his political enemies, including a sitting member of Congress, is putting downballot Republican candidates on the defensive.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Biden administration forgives student loans for 60,000 public service workers
item.description: The Biden administration announced Thursday that it is forgiving the student loans for more than 60,000 public service workers. The move gives $4.5 billion in debt relief to teachers, firefighters and other eligible workers who have been paying on their loans for more than 10 years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Harris Fox News interview draws sharp reactions on both sides
item.description: “If she wins, it will be because of that answer … which is ‘I’m not Joe Biden,’” columnist Chris Cillizza noted.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance says 'no,' he does not think Trump lost in 2020
item.description: Vance has faced repeated questions on the campaign trail about whether Trump lost in 2020. On Wednesday, he gave his most direct answer to date, answering "no" in response to a reporter's question.
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: DENIED: How one Tampa Bay couple recovers after Hurricane Helene
item.description: Floridians across the coast and beyond are feeling overwhelmed with the FEMA application process and denial while struggling on the road to recovery, calling this a “nightmare” situation.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida elections officials seek flexibility on voting rules after hurricanes
item.description: Florida's recent hurricanes could make it difficult for voters in affected areas to cast their ballots in the upcoming election unless Gov. Ron DeSantis waives and modifies some rules, county election supervisors said in a letter to state officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lorena Biasotti, CCSD school board candidate, discusses priorities ahead of election
item.description: Lorena Biasotti, a mother of four and candidate for Clark County School District (CCSD) Board of Trustees, spoke about her platform on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk has given $75 million, so far, to put Donald Trump back in the White House
item.description: Elon Musk is among the largest financial supporters of Donald Trump's reelection efforts, new FEC filings show. He and two other megadonors collectively gave $300 million to support Trump.
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel, MPR News

item.title: Eric Adams welcomes new administration members amid wave of resignations
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Some new faces are joining Mayor Eric Adams' administration as it works to stabilize City Hall after a series of high-level exits. A new city schools chancellor and a new deputy mayor for public safety are taking the reins.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Hennepin County achieves 'functional zero' status in ending veteran homelessness
item.description: The designation from several federal agencies indicates a community has brought down the number of veterans experiencing homelessness to a very low level and continues to sustain the effort.
item.dc:creator: Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: Trump uses interview on economics to promote tariffs and riff on his favorite themes
item.description: Donald Trump has seized on an opening to sound his frequent argument that imposing huge tariffs on foreign goods would amount to an economic elixir — one he claims would raise enormous sums for the government, protect U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota city says Trump campaign still owes more than $200,000 for July rally
item.description: Local officials say former President Donald Trump’s campaign still owes a central Minnesota city nearly $209,000 for services related to a July campaign rally. The Minnesota Star Tribune reports that the bill is for services beyond what St. Cloud normally provides.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Nevada DMV fast-tracks credentials for potential voters
item.description: The Nevada Department of Motor Vehicles is seeking to move potential voters to the front of the line starting next week and is reminding new residents they can surrender their out-of-state ID.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Eric Adams is 'working well through the chaos': Gov. Hochul
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul offered support for embattled New York City Mayor Eric Adams during the Columbus Day Parade in Manhattan on Monday. "Told him to bring in new blood… he's doing that… working well through the chaos," she told PIX11 News.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Election 2024: Races to watch in New Jersey
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PX11) -- It's been one of the most notable years for New Jersey politics in recent memory — indictments, upsets, and loss are just some themes of what has been a year that could shape the Garden State's political future for years to come.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Harris helps pack aid boxes for North Carolina hurricane victims ahead of Sunday campaign push
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris on Saturday helped pack diapers into boxes of personal care products destined for North Carolina hurricane victims, agreeing with one helper who said “it takes a village.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump hears at a Latino campaign event from someone who lived in the US illegally
item.description: Minutes after Donald Trump delivered his standard warnings about drug dealers and criminals illegally crossing the border during a Saturday campaign event, the former president heard from someone who was once in the country illegally but now plans to vote for him.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Henderson City Council candidate Dr. Monica Larson discusses government transparency, public safety, and housing
item.description: Henderson City Council candidate Dr. Monica Larson joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide Friday to discuss issues she believes need to be addressed in the city.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michigan newspaper issues correction after Trump claims he won Man of Year award
item.description: A Michigan newspaper issued a correction after former President Donald Trump cited the paper at a speech in Detroit claiming he was Michigan’s “Man of the Year” in 2023.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5
